Container.Name: motif
Container.Overview:
Automated theme song orchestration for Plex via [b]ThemerrDB[/b].
motif syncs ThemerrDB daily, downloads theme songs from YouTube, and places them as theme.mp3 sidecar files in your Plex movie/show folders so Plex picks them up automatically. Includes a CRT-styled web UI at port 5309 with library browsing, manual overrides, dry-run mode, and per-section management for libraries (Movies, 4K, TV, Anime, etc).
[b]Path model:[/b] motif uses a single /data mount that mirrors what Plex sees. After first boot, you'll choose where to write theme files from the /settings page in the UI (typically /data/media/themes). All settings are saved to /config/motif.yaml and persist across container restarts.
[b]Before first run:[/b] 1) Drop a YouTube cookies.txt file into your appdata folder (Netscape format from the "Get cookies.txt LOCALLY" extension) 2) Set your Plex token below 3) After the container starts, visit /settings and set the THEMES DIRECTORY (e.g. /data/media/themes) 4) The first sync runs in DRY-RUN mode by default — disable from the UI banner once you've reviewed the planned actions
Container.Requires: A YouTube cookies.txt file and a valid Plex token. See the project README for the cookies workflow.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port for the motif web UI. Pick anything free on your host.

Config.Name: Config (appdata)
Config.Description: motif's persistent state: SQLite DB, motif.yaml configuration file, cookies.txt, generated session key. MUST be writable by user 99:100. Drop your YouTube cookies.txt here as cookies.txt.

Config.Name: Data (mirrors Plex)
Config.Description: Single unified data mount. Should mirror what your Plex container sees as /data — typically /mnt/user/data on Unraid. motif reads/writes inside this single tree so hardlinks work for free. After first boot, configure the THEMES DIRECTORY from the /settings page in the UI (typically /data/media/themes).

Config.Name: Plex URL
Config.Description: Plex Media Server URL reachable from inside this container. Use the LAN IP of your Plex host. Example: http://10.0.1.98:32400. Can be changed later from /settings.

Config.Name: Plex token
Config.Description: Plex auth token. Find at https://support.plex.tv/articles/204059436-finding-an-authentication-token-x-plex-token/ — log into Plex Web, look at any item's XML metadata, copy X-Plex-Token from the URL. Can be changed later from /settings.

Config.Name: Trust forward-auth header
Config.Description: Set 'true' ONLY if motif sits behind Authentik or another forward-auth proxy that injects X-Authentik-Username. Leave 'false' for direct LAN access — otherwise an attacker who can hit the container directly could spoof the header.

Config.Name: Section exclude (optional)
Config.Description: Comma-separated Plex section TITLES to exclude from management (e.g. 'Home Movies,Music Videos'). Leave empty unless you need this — you can also flip per-section management from the /libraries page in the UI.

Config.Name: Section include / whitelist (optional)
Config.Description: Optional whitelist. When set, ONLY these section titles are managed. Comma-separated, e.g. 'Movies,4K Movies,TV Shows,Anime'. Overrides the exclude list.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Container timezone — affects log timestamps and the cron schedule (which is interpreted in UTC inside motif but reported in local time in the UI).
